Transcrição Completa: 42 Minutes of Sales Training That Will Explode Your Sales in 2026
[00:00] Every prospect that does not buy comes down to the fear of change. [música]
[00:05] Pretend you and I are in the elevator and I say, "What do you do?" Give me your 30 second elevator pitch.
[00:10] I take people from kind of being a slave to bills and things that they rent all the time and turn it into ownership so you can live a better life.
[00:18] What do you sell?
[00:19] 30 second pitch. Hold it.
[00:21] I increase people's net worth tremendously over 30 years.
[00:25] What do you sell?
[00:26] Solar.
[00:27] 30 second pitch. I help people find a solution to lower their overall cost on energy and help them find ownership solutions and increase their equity on their homes.
[00:38] Somebody that's not in solar raise your hand. What do you sell?
[00:42] Like product service roofing.
[00:44] Roofing. Okay. I want you four to come up here on stage with me real quick.
[00:50] So if I asked a competitor of yours the same exact question. I said, "Hey, you know what do you do? Give me your best elevator pitch for your industry. What do you think they might say?"
[00:59] Protect all your assets and save you a lot of money.
[01:02] Okay. What about you? What are your competitors? I ask the same question. What do you think they might say?
[01:07] You're paying too much for your electricity and we can do it for way cheaper.
[01:11] What about you? What would they say?
[01:13] Utility rates over the long term so it doesn't increase with inflation.
[01:17] And you what would they say?
[01:19] Help people lower their costs of their overall expenses.
[01:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Commodity Trap - Todos soam iguais"
NÚMEROS: 4 vendedores comparados
VISUAL: Layout comparativo mostrando 4 pitches de vendas similares, demonstrando falta de diferenciação no mercado
[01:25] Okay. So what I think I heard all of you just say to yourself is that in your prospect's mind you all kind of sound the same now? Could that be a problem for you?
[01:35] So what's going to make you stand out? How do you stand out from everybody else saying the same thing? What's going to make you actually different?
[01:43] Now, if you really thought I want you to put yourself in your prospect's shoes, like don't be the salesperson right now. Go in your prospect's shoes that you talk to every day. In reality, do they know all the differences between what you do and your competitors do? Yes or no?
[01:57] No.
[01:58] So, in their mind, would you say that you kind of all sound the same to you? Okay. So, what's going to separate you from everybody else?
[02:06] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "POV - Perception of Value"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Conceito central escrito em whiteboard, destacado como princípio fundamental de diferenciação
[02:08] This is what I want you to write down. I'm going to put it here on this board. I want you to write down POV. So write down POV. This is really important for you right now.
[02:18] Okay. So what is the POV? Now POV stands for the perception of the value.
[02:24] You know, I hear a lot of sales people say, "Well, I got to show them more value." The problem is is that all your competitors are showing them the same value. Would I be right? Yes or no?
[02:33] Yes.
[02:34] They're showing them the same value. They say the same things. So, it's how the prospect perceives that value that separates you from closing that sale or not.
[02:44] How do they perceive the value? You ever seen like products or services that are really, really good, but hardly anybody's buying them? Raise your hand if that's you. If you've ever seen that.
[02:55] Okay. On the flip, only half of you. Am I in the right room? Okay. Okay. And on the flip side, have you guys seen products that are like really, really bad, but they sell like crazy? Raise your hand.
[03:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Produtos bons vs Produtos ruins que vendem"
NÚMEROS: 50% da audiência levantou a mão
VISUAL: Comparação paradoxal entre qualidade de produto e sucesso de vendas, demonstrando importância da percepção
[03:10] Well, what's the difference? That company or those salespeople know how to frame that offer. They know how to the perception of the value far more than other companies. The perceived value is what matters. And that's what we're going to focus on today.
[03:26] So, what do I mean by framing your offer, framing your service? Okay. So, I want you to get the family photo in your mind. Picture your own family photo. You got your siblings, your parents, maybe you got your kids, the one on your desk, your mantle. You guys have the family picture in your mind.
[03:42] Okay. Imagine two of those pictures from your family. They're exactly the same picture. Now you have to go out and buy what? Two frames. Two different frames. Yeah.
[03:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Analogia da Moldura - Frame Your Offer"
NÚMEROS: 2 fotos idênticas, 2 molduras diferentes
VISUAL: Comparação visual entre moldura de ouro imaculada vs moldura suja de feira, mesma foto, percepções diferentes
[03:54] So, let's say one frame is like gold, immaculate, like looks amazing. Then the other frame for the photo, you go to the flea market. It's got cobwebs on it. It's dirty. It's like looks beat up. Same exact picture. Which one are you getting?
[04:08] The one that's framed better.
[04:10] Are you picking up what I'm starting to put down here? So, it's all how you frame what you're doing that's going to determine if they buy or if they do not buy. You get me?
[04:21] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Como treinar 163+ indústrias diferentes"
NÚMEROS: 163 verticais diferentes segundo Forbes
VISUAL: Referência à Forbes Magazine, amplitude de indústrias treinadas, conceito de aplicação universal
[04:23] So, the biggest question that companies and salespeople from all industries ask me, ask my team is how are you guys able to train every industry on the planet? According to Forbes magazine, there's 163 different verticals.
[04:36] Now, in those verticals, there are subcategories of those. Who sells like home improvement? For example, home. Okay, home improvement home service. That's like our fifth biggest industry we train.
[04:48] Now, in that, there's doors, there's windows, there's cabinets, there's garages, there's HVAC, there's plumbing, all these different industries.
[04:56] And so, the question is, how do you able how are you able to duplicate in every single industry to get those type of results?
[05:04] And to be clear, companies and salespeople do not come to us because they believe that I know their product, their service, their industry better than them. They come to me because they know I know how to sell that or train that and how to frame their product, their service, their industry much better than them. And that's what we're going to focus on today.
[05:25] Now, I'm going to ask you this question. How many of you are ready to actually take your sales your sales ability to a level that most people never get to? Raise your hand if that's you.
[05:35] All right, that's what I make sure I'm in the right room. And once I show you how and what you need to do, how many of you are actually going to act and actually do what I tell you to do to get there? Raise your hand if that's you.
[05:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Selling: TO people vs FOR people"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Contraste filosófico fundamental, mudança de mentalidade de manipulação para serviço
[05:49] Okay, I'm going to ask you this very, very important question. And depending on how you answer this will depend on how your sales career will go. Whether you own the business and you want to scale more, whether you sell, is selling something you do to people or is selling something you do for people?
[06:05] Is selling something you do to people or is selling something you do for people? Let me know.
[06:10] For people.
[06:11] For people. Because if we have a belief system that we are doing it to them, like it's something bad, like we're manipulating them, like it's a bad thing, how confident are you going to be to help them overcome a concern or objection and actually close that deal?
[06:26] Not very confident. Right? So, anytime you try to close, you're going to have what? Anxiety. And you're going to feel nervous. Right?
[06:35] Now, why do we have that? Why does that actually happen to us? Okay? Why is that important for you guys to actually understand?
[06:42] I want you to think about this from this point of view. How many of you have ever talked to a salesperson and you had some massive problems? You knew you should buy what they were offering. Like you knew it. You knew that if you bought it, it was going to help you. It's going to solve your problems, get whatever you want in your life. You knew all that, yet you still didn't do it. Raise your hand if that's you.
[07:05] Raise your hand if that's you. That should be everybody.
[07:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Padrões de Comportamento do Comprador"
NÚMEROS: 100% da audiência levantou a mão
VISUAL: Demonstração universal de padrões de resistência à compra, mesmo quando sabemos que precisamos
[07:10] And how many of you I'll give you an example. How many of you have been to an event like this? Maybe you got some great advice. Maybe you could have got a training program or something to invest in that you knew if you did it, you would get all these crazy results and you would get where you want yet you still didn't do it. Raise your hand if that's you.
[07:28] Now, for a lot of people, that's called a what? That's called a pattern.
[07:33] So, if you have patterns that, let's say, prevent you from buying products and services that you know you need to get where you want to go, would that also imply that your prospects have the same patterns and that's why they're not buying from you? Yes or no?
[07:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "O Cérebro: Sobrevivência vs Prosperidade"
NÚMEROS: Milhões de anos de evolução
VISUAL: Conceito de design cerebral primitivo focado em sobrevivência, não em crescimento
[07:50] Would that would that imply that? Okay. Now, why does that happen? You see, the brain often comes up with excuses on why we do something or not do something even we know we should do it.
[08:02] And why does that happen? Because the brain, you know, whenever God put the first humans here on the earth millions of years ago, whenever that was, that's debatable. The brain was never designed for us to thrive. The brain was actually designed for us to survive, to be safe. Is everybody getting this?
[08:22] Okay. That's how we're wired. So, anytime there's something new or something unknown or there's change, the brain plays tricks on us. The brain tries to convince us not to do something even though we know what to do.
[08:36] Now, congratulations, you're alive, right? But why do we not buy something? Why do we not do something? Why does our prospects not do that?
[08:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "FEAR OF CHANGE - Raiz de Todas as Objeções"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Conceito central destacado como causa raiz universal de resistência à compra
[08:47] It's because it's something that's unknown, something unfamiliar, and they have to give you money before they see what's on the other side of what? Change.
[08:57] Every prospect that does not buy comes down to the core of every human being has a fear of change.
[09:04] So every objection you get, it's too expensive. We don't need it. I need to talk to my spouse. I need to think it over. We need to talk with the board for six months. We'll push it down the road. Now's not the right time. I need to do more research. I'm not interested. We already have it.
[09:19] Every objection you get, every reason the prospect does not buy comes down to the root cause, the fear of change.
[09:28] Now, whose job is it to help them overcome that fear of change so they get what they want? Whose job is that? Let me know. Is that our job?
[09:38] Okay, I'm making sure I'm with the right people here. Okay, good.
[09:42] So, what causes a prospect to close down emotionally? Do you know?
[09:47] A lack of trust.
[09:48] But what causes a lack of trust?
[09:50] Lack of rapport.
[09:51] What causes a lack of rapport? What does rapport mean?
[09:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "O que causa fechamento emocional?"
NÚMEROS: 30 segundos até fight-or-flight, 45 minutos até visita
VISUAL: Timeline de reações defensivas do prospect, gatilhos emocionais
[09:57] Hey, how you doing today? I'm doing good. What's this all about? Anytime a prospect closes down or gives you an objection, did they plan that out before you got there?
[10:07] Did they like, you know, before that solar rep knocks on my door in about 45 minutes, I think I'm going to go into fight or flight mode about 30 seconds in and say, we're good. We're not interested.
[10:18] Or I think, you know, when that salesperson comes out to sell me windows, I think like I'm going to like put my arms in. I'm just going to give them like one-word answers. I'm going to seem like really closed off and at the end I'm going to say, I need about four other bids. Come back later. Did they plan that out?
[10:33] Or was it something you said or something you asked or how you asked your tone that triggered their guard up that triggered that reaction?
[10:43] Oh, okay. We're reverse engineering now. Once we start to understand this, what does that give us an advantage on? Now, we know how to prevent that from happening, don't we? Is that what you want to learn?
[10:55] Yes.
[10:56] Okay. That's why I came here.
[10:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "NPQ Identity Frame"
NÚMEROS: 2-3 perguntas de conexão antes de usar
VISUAL: Framework de construção de identidade do comprador, técnica de posicionamento psicológico
[11:00] So, I'm going to show you a few things. This is kind of fun. Probably going to have to kick me off stage here. Going to go over time. I always do. Okay. So, let's go.
[11:08] So, I'm going to just give you some examples here. Okay. Now, I'm going to call out a few different ones. I'll just show you to do this. So, how do you build them into the identity of the person who becomes a buyer? That's called an NPQ identity frame. Write this down.
[11:23] Now, this is why so many of you, you see something I do on Instagram or LinkedIn or Facebook, like a 60-second reel, and you use it, you get all excited, and it doesn't work because you don't know what to do before to set it up. You don't know what to do if it doesn't weigh in.
[11:39] That's the dabbler. Who's ready to stop being a dabbler? Raise your hand. Okay. Who's committed to master? Keep your hand up. Okay. All right.
[11:48] So, this is important here. You would never use this at the start of a conversation. So, this would not be the first thing you would say. If you do, you're going to get slapped in the face by the velvet hammer. That hurts really bad. Don't use this as the first question.

Anyways, what were you hoping could happen by possibly working something out?

[17:05] Afraid of change. We got we got to we got somebody starting to pick this up. They're afraid of change. That's the core, right? They have problems. They know they need to solve them. But the brain anytime we feel like something is unknown or unsure or we haven't done it before, the brain, it's like the brain's last attempt to keep us in the status quo safe.
[17:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Cérebro: Sobreviver vs Prosperar"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Reforço do conceito de design cerebral para segurança, não crescimento

[21:11] Yeah. Yeah. Like I said, I'm pretty healthy now though.

[22:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TOM CORRETO: Concerned vs Curious"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Comparação de tons vocais, quando usar cada tipo, impacto no prospect
[22:30] So the some did something recently happen question. Okay. You want me to show you? Absolutely.
[22:38] So did did something like recently happen or or what caused you to feel like maybe you need more coverage? What am I starting to see? What tone did I use there?
[22:52] Concerned.
[22:53] Concerned. I use the curious tone which in other parts of the conversation I want to use. But if I want to see doubt and open up the prospect because do we all understand typically when we ask questions to find problems like hey what are some of your challenges or what keep you awake at night like what are some problems you have? Do we understand that our prospects kind of know where those questions lead? Yes or no?
[23:18] Yes. Yes or no?
[23:20] So that's why they start to go surface level because they recognize the pattern. See our brains recognize patterns. Your survival party brain recognizes patterns.
[23:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "INTERRUPÇÃO DE PADRÕES"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Conceito de reconhecimento de padrões pelo cérebro, frases que prospects já ouviram milhares de vezes
[23:32] So when we say things like just following up, just circling back, just checking in. How many times have they heard that from every salesperson trying to sell them anything and they recognize the pattern? Oh, not interested. It's a pattern.
[23:47] How do I interrupt the pattern? My tone.
[23:51] Now, probably what I would more than likely do, let's say if I found out you had like a like a work policy of 50,000 because that could happen, right? And a lot of sales people and insurance, they struggle. They like, well, they already got a policy. They're not going to need it. But here's what I do. You just got to reframe it.
[24:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "REFRAME: Já tem apólice de $50k"
NÚMEROS: $50,000 apólice de trabalho existente
VISUAL: Técnica de reframe para prospects que já têm cobertura, como criar gap mesmo assim
[24:12] Like, so help me understand like you you've already got this $50,000 work policy. What's causing you to feel like that might not be enough?
[24:22] What am I doing now? What he does is do what? Well, yeah, I got the policy, but and now he tells me why he doesn't feel it's enough. And more importantly, who does he tell? Himself.
[24:36] You see how I'm showing you how to start reframing some of your questions? I'm what? Interrupting the pattern. Now I get him to defend himself on why he's looking for more because he already has something.
[24:48] But if I said it, so you've I mean I don't understand like you got this $50,000 policy. What's causing you to feel like it might not be enough, man? Oh, I don't know. Like I'm just always looking.
[24:59] I said the same words. It's how I say the words. Are you getting this? Yeah. Okay, good. Good job, man. Thanks, man. [aplausos]
[25:08] Now, there's some other things I would show you there, but like that's that's a start.
[25:13] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "PROBLEM AWARENESS QUESTIONS - 3 Razões"
NÚMEROS: 3 razões principais listadas
VISUAL: Framework escrito em whiteboard, estrutura de descoberta de problemas
[25:15] Now, this is called these are called problem awareness questions. I'm probably going to run out of time, so you're going to have to throw me off here. These are called problem awareness questions.
[25:24] Now, why do I need to master problem awareness questions? Can we get this on the board when you guys have time? There's three reasons why I need to ask problem awareness questions.
[25:34] Reason number one, what are the prospect's real problems? Notice I didn't say problem. I said problems. Problems. Problems. You know what happens most of the time? Prospect tells you a problem and you do what? Oh, well, let me show you how to solve that.
[25:54] And once you do that, how big is that gap? Maybe here. What if I didn't take the bait? Yeah, that's a big problem. That's the biggest reason why companies or space come to us for sure. What else do you feel like might be going on?
[26:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "MÚLTIPLOS PROBLEMAS = GAP MAIOR"
NÚMEROS: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 problemas diferentes
VISUAL: Visualização de gap pequeno vs gap grande, impacto de múltiplos problemas na urgência de compra
[26:10] So I want to find multiple problems. Why do I want to find multiple problems? Because that builds a much bigger gap that causes them to feel like they need me more, right?
[26:20] Because if I don't build that gap and I only find one problem, what's to say that let's say marketing agents, they're like, you know, maybe we can just figure this out on our own. But if I help them find two, three, four, five, six different reasons why they want to change, it's pretty damn hard.
[26:37] The gap is so big that they of course have to go with you because no other salesperson knows even how to fill the gap.
[26:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "COST-BASED vs RESULTS-BASED Thinking"
NÚMEROS: $185 vs $130 (exemplo anterior)
VISUAL: Contraste entre foco em preço vs foco em resultados/proteção familiar
[26:46] So instead of like lowering the price like with the insurance, see how I'm doing that is getting him back into cost-based thinking. Not so is the So help me understand is the cheapest price the most important to you or actually having enough coverage to like pay off the house for sending the kids so she's not forced to go get like a second job when you pass.
[27:07] Now, what did I just take them out of? Price or cost based thinking to results based thinking.
[27:14] You guys want to master this? It's a lot of fun. You can help a lot of people. It sucks not being able to help people you want to help if you don't know how to communicate? Yeah. Yeah. Okay. Good.
[27:26] So, and number two, what is the root cause of the problem and how the how are the problems affecting them even personally?
[27:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "GENERIC FRAMES - Exemplos"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Lista de frames genéricos que precisam ser adaptados por indústria
[27:37] Now, these are some generic frames right here. I'm just going to show you a few of these because we got a fly here. So, these are generic. You're not going to like just use generic frames, okay? Like we have I have to show you how to tie it into your industry. That's why people come to us. Okay?
[27:54] So, help me understand. Do you 100% love the results you've been getting? Not I wouldn't ask that for solar. It wouldn't make any sense.
[28:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "FRAME: 100% Love Results?"
NÚMEROS: 100%, 5 anos com Merrill Lynch
VISUAL: Exemplo de frame para serviços financeiros/investimentos
[28:05] Let's say if I sold financial services like investing. So, you've been with Merrill Price the last five years. Like, are you 100% in love with the returns you've been getting?
[28:16] Well, I wouldn't say it's 100%. Not 100%. What? What don't you like?
[28:22] Why did I put 100% there? Cuz nobody is happy with anything 100%. Does everybody understand the brain works that way?
[28:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "PSICOLOGIA: Ninguém ama nada 100%"
NÚMEROS: 6 meses de relacionamento
VISUAL: Analogia de relacionamento romântico, evolução de percepção ao longo do tempo
[28:32] Think about the person you're dating with or married with. Now, the first couple of months, you loved them. They were floating on cloud nine. They could do no wrong. Six months later, you found what? Things you didn't like.
[28:46] Nobody likes anything 100%. You see how I'm framing it to focus the brain on not do they like it, but do they like it 100%. That's a whole different conversation.
[28:58] You see what I'm doing? I'm building the gap automatically. Now, what if I say like this? So, do you do 100% like the thing you're doing? Yeah, it's pretty good.
[29:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TOM = TUDO"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Demonstração de como mesmo tom errado destrói a melhor pergunta
[29:10] The tone the tone separate. So, so help me understand like do you 100% like the results they've been getting for you?
[29:20] What does my tone sound like? I'm concerned for you. I know something that you might not know. That triggers what? Doubt.
[29:30] No. Concerned tone triggers doubt. Curious tone triggers like he's genuinely curious. Okay, you guys are killing me. You're just making that up, right? Okay.
[29:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "REAL ESTATE FRAME"

[29:54] So, most real estate agents like, "Well, why do you want to sell the home?" Blah blah blah blah. Surface level answer. No, I'm not going to do that.

[30:19] Well, we like it, but... Bam. Bam. Oh, it's so simple once you learn the frames. So hard when you don't. I know you guys want to. Okay.

[31:07] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SOLAR FRAME - Rate Hikes"
NÚMEROS: N/A

[31:24] Well, no. Of course not. But see how I paced it out. What are those little periods there? Do I just not know punctuation?
[31:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "VERBAL PAUSE ..."
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Técnica de pausa verbal representada por pontos, timing na comunicação
[31:34] That's called a verbal pause. Write down verbal pause. You have so much to learn. So little time.
[31:41] I mean, hold on. I mean, you've been with XYZ company the last seven years. I mean, they're fairly decent. I mean, what's caused you to feel like you might want to look at someone else?
[31:53] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "FAIRLY DECENT - Posicionamento Neutro"
NÚMEROS: 7 anos com empresa, 12 anos exemplo
VISUAL: Técnica de não elogiar nem criticar competidor, posição neutra estratégica
[31:55] What am I doing? Fairly. So, I don't want to say, "Oh, you have you're with a great company." Because that just reinforces their frame. I should stay. I don't want to say that company sucks because now they know you're trying to sell them.
[32:10] So, I'm going to say fairly decent because I'm in the middle. But notice how my tone shifts. Yeah. I mean, you've been with them the last 12 years. I mean, they're fairly decent. I mean, what's I mean, they're not too bad. I mean, what's causing?
[32:25] You guys starting to get this? Raise your hand if you want to master selling. Okay. All right. Let's keep going here. Oh, we're just Oh gosh. Okay. I wish I could have you master everything in 97 minutes. Dog gone it. Okay.
[32:39] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "5 TIPOS DE TOM ESSENCIAIS"
NÚMEROS: 5 tons, 93% vs atualizado 72% não-verbal, 28% palavras
VISUAL: Estatística atualizada sobre comunicação, importância de tonalidade
[32:41] Now, let's keep going here. Now, this is important here. There are five types of tone you must master if you want to sell a lot more than you are now.
[32:50] How many of you have read a book that says 93% of communication is your non-verbal? Raise your hand. Every damn sales book in the planet just copied and pasted that. I swear, you know, it comes actually from the 1940s by a survey that they did. It's not even accurate.
[33:05] They've redone my friend, I have a friend, body language expert called Mark Bowden, teaches a lot of EU parliament how to use body language and stuff, but they've updated this.
[33:15] So 93% of your communication making the sale is your non-verbal, your tone, your body language. 7%'s the words. Holy that's crazy.
[33:24] Now, they've updated that study. 72% of you making the sale is your tonality, how you say it, how you ask the question, and your body language. About 28% is still the words.
[33:38] Words matter. Questions matter. Okay, we might want to learn this thing.
[33:43] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TOM CURIOSO - Exemplos"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Demonstrações de tom curioso em diferentes contextos de vendas
[33:45] So, there's a curious tone. So, walk me through. What do you guys do now to generate new leads and clients? So, walk me through like where do you typically invest your funds just so I can see how we could possibly help you? Okay. It's different things I could do.
[34:02] So your bill, can you tell me a little bit more about your bill? I I know in the summer I I know that most of the year it's pretty low, but what what have they been making you pay lately?
[34:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "DOWNPLAY para UPPLAY - Solar"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Técnica de minimizar para prospect maximizar, psicologia reversa
[34:17] So, tell me a little bit about your bill. I I know a lot of the year it's pretty low, but what have they been making you pay lately?
[34:25] What am I doing? Solar guys, tell me a little bit about your bill. I know a lot of the year is pretty low, but what have they been making you pay lately?
[34:33] Why would I do that? Why would I say most of the year it's pretty low? Because what are they used to from sales people that knock on their door? Solar. Oh, the bill's really high. No, it's not.
[34:46] Even though it is defensive mechanism because they know you're trying to sell them something. So, why do I downplay it? They upplay it.
[34:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "MISMATCH TECHNIQUE"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Conceito de mismatch - fazer o oposto do esperado para desativar defesas
[34:57] That's called mismatch. I know a lot of the year is pretty low, but what have they been what have they been making you pay lately?
[35:05] What tone? Concern tone. Making you pay. Does anybody like to be made or forced to do anything? Oh, what am I starting to shift there?
[35:17] I'm starting to get them to not like who's been forcing them to pay. But if I'm like, "How much are you paying?" Doesn't do anything. No doubt. Lots of objections.
[35:27] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "PREVENIR vs LIDAR com Objeções"
NÚMEROS: 10x mais vendas
VISUAL: Filosofia de prevenção vs manejo, multiplicador de resultados
[35:29] Who loves handling objections? I hate objections. I just like to prevent them from happening in their mind. It's so much easier to sell. You can sell like 10 times more if you just prevent objections.
[35:42] You ever heard sales trainers like the more objections they give you, the more interested they are. How the f does that make any sense? [risos]
[35:50] What about all the laydowns you got that gave you no objections? They seem pretty easy to sell. So the more objections I get, the more they're going to that just they just said that to make you feel better about yourself.
[36:03] Everybody realizes that we we all smart. This is a smart room, right? We all know this, right?
[36:09] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "VERDADE: Mais objeções = Menos vendas"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Contradição de "sabedoria" tradicional de vendas, lógica correta
[36:11] Okay, write that down. The more objections you get, the less likely they're going to buy cuz they're triggered. Their guards up.
[36:19] I'd rather prevent the objection from happening in their brain. Cuz who causes the objection? The salesperson causes the objection, not the prospect. We all know this, right?
[36:29] Okay, you're starting to get this. All right. How am I going to get this? That's too expensive. I can't How much time do I have left team back there? Am I getting kicked off? I have 20. I have 10 minutes. 25. Can I take 23? Okay.
[36:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "OBJEÇÃO: It's Too Expensive"

[36:47] All right. So, that's too expensive. Anybody get this objection? Okay. I need a lady boss to volunteer. Hey, lady boss. Come on up. Got to get the lady. [aplausos] Let's go, lady. [aplausos]
[37:05] Oh my god. All these guys. How do you do How do you do it? What do you sell, Jennifer?
[37:12] Home transformation.

[37:26] Okay. So you help people like save time like busy executives so they're not like sitting there all day Saturday organizing their closet like hating life. Exactly.
[37:35] That sounds a lot better. I like that for sure. Okay. [risos] That's exact. That's what I said.
[37:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "ROLE PLAY: Too Expensive"

[37:54] I would probably say sure. [risos]
[37:58] Hold on. Are you Hold on. Are you honest? Are you going to buy into their story?
[38:05] No.
[38:06] Do they need you?
[38:07] Yes.
[38:08] If Yes, they do. Yes, they do. Is selling something you're doing to them or for them?
[38:15] For them.
[38:16] Why?
[38:17] Because they need it.
[38:18] Why?
[38:19] Because they're overwhelmed.
[38:21] Yeah. And stressed. Yeah. And everyone wants more time. Yeah.
[38:26] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "NÃO COMPRE A HISTÓRIA DELES"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Princípio fundamental - não aceitar desculpas, oferecer valor real
[38:28] You can't buy into their story anymore. Do you understand that? Because you offer them no effing value if you do. It's true.
[38:38] Is that why you got into what you're doing so you could help people? Yes. That's exactly what I did.
[38:44] Stop buying into their story. Because we all know that's a story, right? We all know they need you.
[38:51] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "NPQ COMPARATIVE FRAME"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Framework para sair de cost-based para results-based thinking
[38:53] Okay. So, what could I do maybe instead? Let me show you something if I have time. Okay, let's see. I think I wrote this out for you guys somewhere. Let's see. Oh, yes.
[39:04] Now, so that prospect that gave you that objection, that tells me they're what? What does that tell me? I didn't do my job. I didn't sell the value.
[39:15] Well, no, it has nothing to do with the value. It's the perception of the value. Yeah, correct. How do they perceive the value?
[39:24] Okay, let's write this down. This is called a comparative frame. NPQ comparative frame. Because right now, this prospect that gave you that objection is in what way of thinking? Price or cost-based thinking. So, how do I take them out of that to the end result? You want to learn?
[39:43] Yes.
[39:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "COMPARATIVE FRAME SCRIPT - Home Organization"
NÚMEROS: 6 horas economizadas todo fim de semana
VISUAL: Script completo de reframe comparativo, mudança de foco de custo para resultado
[39:46] Oh, yeah. Yeah. I mean, in in reality, if you really thought about it, like which is I mean, really, which is costing you more? I mean, is it costing you more to like get the funding together, you know, we come in, we organize your entire house, and you're saving about like 6 hours every weekend so you can actually focus on your work. Or is it more expensive to do nothing at all and the clothes are all over the place and you're having to spend your entire weekend working in reality, which is costing you more?
[40:20] Concern tone. See the shift down? Why? Why would I shift down at a concerned tone at the end? Cuz I'm effing concerned for you. Actually, that's why. Cuz that would really suck to have to be spend the whole weekend.
[40:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Selling = FOR them, not TO them"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Filosofia central reforçada, propósito ético de técnicas de tonalidade
[40:37] Does everybody understand this? Like selling is not something you do to them. It's for them. So, if I'm mastering my tonality to get their guard down, am I doing that to manipulate them or to actually get their guard down so I can freaking help them?
[40:52] I'm doing it for them. These are not We don't manipulate people. This is what selling is. You're doing it for them, not to them.
[41:01] If I can't get their guard down, what's the likelihood they're going to buy? Unlikely. If I keep them in price or cost space, what's unlikely they're going to buy? Unlikely.
[41:11] If I don't learn how to do this, I offered them no value. I wasted their time. You don't want to waste their time anymore, do you? No. Why? Doesn't help either of you. That's why you're in sales, right?
[41:25] Okay. So, you see that reframe? Okay. See, now I'm getting you to think what? Which is costing me more? Now, I'm not going to stop there. I'm going to go right here after this. Okay. You want to try? You'll have to look at it. It takes a while to learn this stuff.
[41:41] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "PRÁTICA: Jennifer lendo script"

[42:50] All right. Give it up. All right. Good. Good job. [aplausos] Good job. Good job.
[42:57] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TWEAKS: Descrição Detalhada Necessária"
NÚMEROS: 8 horas/dia trabalhando (exemplo)
VISUAL: Feedback de melhoria - necessidade de pintar cenário vívido, não frases genéricas
[42:59] Now I'm going to show you some tweaks. Okay. Cuz that's still kind of surface level changing your life. They hear all this time. It's like it's like in one out there.
[43:09] I mean, in reality, which is more expensive? Is it more expensive for like you get the funding together, we come in, we completely organize your closets, we completely do all of the clothes, we completely like do everything on the floors and you're not having to be forced to spend your entire weekend like slaving away. Or is it more expensive to do nothing at all and you're working eight hours a day just to like mow the lawn and blah blah blah blah in reality? Which is more expensive?
[43:39] See, I got to describe it. I got to frame it a little bit like that would really suck to have to do all that work if they told me that, right? I got to pull that out of them, right? I don't make up a number. I pull that out. Does that help?
[43:53] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "INTENTION FRAME"
NÚMEROS: $10,000 investimento
VISUAL: Próximo framework após comparative, foco em intenção real vs manter dinheiro
[43:55] Now, after I do that, I don't just stop. This is called an intention frame. So, how much of your services? How much do they cost?
[44:02] 2500 a day.
[44:04] So, how many days do you need to come in? Two days, three days. On average, four days. Let's say four days. 10 grand. Yeah. Okay.
[44:14] So, right up to in reality, which is more expensive? Because I'm sure it's not your real intention just to keep the $10,000 in your bank account for the rest of your life and hope and pray it just stays there forever with all the inflation.
[44:28] I'm sure your real intention is to never have to work on the weekends again in your life. So, if you really thought about what's going to put you in the best position where you can save that time and be with your family and have somebody else do all that work for you, intention.
[44:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "REFRAME: Intenção Real"
NÚMEROS: $10,000 vs tempo com família
VISUAL: Contraste entre guardar dinheiro vs alcançar objetivo real de vida
[44:47] See how I reframed her? What's her real intention? [aplausos]
[44:52] Give me another one. Let's give me another industry over here. What? Business coaching.
[44:58] Okay. Like business consulting, helping them scale, building systems. Yeah.
[45:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "COMPARATIVE FRAME - Business Coaching"
NÚMEROS: $76,000 investimento, $10 milhões/mês meta, 1 ano timeline
VISUAL: Aplicação do mesmo framework para B2B coaching, escalabilidade de negócio
[45:05] Because I'm sure it's I mean, in reality, which is more expensive? Is it more expensive to get your funding together? We come in with our internal team and we build out your systems and you're able to scale to $10 million this month, you know, in the next year, or is it more expensive to do nothing at all? You know, you guys stay with the same systems and you never end up scaling? I mean, in reality, which is more expensive?
[45:30] Because I'm sure it's not your real intention just to keep the $76,000 in your bank account, just hope and pray it like stays there forever, especially with all the inflation going on. I'm sure it's your real intention is to really grow your business where you never have to worry about money again in your life, right?
[45:48] So, what's going to put you in the best position where you never have to worry because you're able to scale?
[45:54] Who wants to master this? Raise your hand. Let's go, baby.
[45:59] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Por que vêm até nós"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Proposta de valor - frameworks aplicáveis universalmente com treinamento adequado
[46:01] This is why people like you guys come to us. Okay, does that help everybody? Thank you very much. Let's go, baby. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. [aplausos]
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
163 verticais de indústria diferentes segundo Forbes Magazine
72% da comunicação de vendas é tonalidade e linguagem corporal (atualizado)
28% são as palavras utilizadas
10x mais vendas ao prevenir objeções vs lidar com elas
93% comunicação não-verbal (estatística antiga dos anos 1940, agora desatualizada)
Home improvement/home service: 5ª maior indústria treinada
Life insurance: maior espaço treinado
Real estate: 2ª maior indústria treinada
$2,500/dia para 4 dias = $10,000 (exemplo home organization)
$185 atual vs $130 desejado (exemplo seguro de vida)
$50,000 apólice de trabalho (exemplo)
$76,000 investimento (exemplo business coaching)
$10 milhões/mês meta de scale
0-30 dias de leads ETO
6 horas economizadas por fim de semana
30 anos de idade (prospect exemplo)
96-100 anos (genética familiar exemplo)
6 meses de follow-up sugerido
2-3 perguntas de conexão antes de identity frame
3-4 quarteirões de distância (exemplo)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
POV (Perception of Value) - Foco em como prospect PERCEBE valor, não apenas mostrar valor
Framing Your Offer - Analogia da moldura: mesmo produto, apresentação diferente = resultados diferentes
NPQ Identity Frame - Construir prospect na identidade de quem compra, não de quem resiste
Problem Awareness Questions - 3 razões:
Descobrir MÚLTIPLOS problemas (não apenas um)
Encontrar causa raiz e impacto pessoal
Construir gap maior = maior urgência
Mismatch Technique - Fazer oposto do esperado para desativar defesas (downplay para upplay)
Comparative Frame - Sair de cost-based para results-based thinking
Intention Frame - Focar na intenção REAL, não em guardar dinheiro
Verbal Pause - Uso estratégico de pausas (...) na comunicação
Pattern Interruption - Quebrar padrões que prospects reconhecem ("just following up", etc.)
Prevenção vs Manejo de Objeções - Prevenir na mente vs lidar depois
ESTRUTURA:
5 Tipos de Tom Essenciais:
Tom Curioso
Tom Preocupado/Concerned
Tom Neutro ("fairly decent")
(2 não especificados no vídeo)
Filosofia Central: Selling FOR people, not TO people
Fear of Change - Raiz de TODAS as objeções:
It's too expensive
We don't need it
Need to talk to spouse
Need to think it over
Need board approval
Push down the road
Not the right time
Need more research
Not interested
Already have it
Cérebro: Projetado para SOBREVIVER, não PROSPERAR
Medo de mudança = mecanismo de sobrevivência
Desconhecido = perigo = resistência
Padrões reconhecidos = defesas ativadas
Processo de Vendas:
2-3 perguntas de conexão
Identity frames
Problem awareness (múltiplos problemas)
Build gap
Comparative frame
Intention frame
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Não revelados valores de faturamento da empresa de treinamento
Exemplos de investimentos: $10k, $76k
Exemplos de economia: $55/mês ($185→$130)
Exemplos de metas: $10M/mês
ROI implícito: 10x mais vendas com prevenção de objeções
INSIGHTS-CHAVE:
Todos os vendedores soam iguais aos prospects
Produtos ruins vendem mais que produtos bons quando bem "framados"
Prospects têm mesmos padrões de resistência que nós
Salesperson CAUSA a objeção, não o prospect
Mais objeções = MENOS chance de venda (contrário ao senso comum)
Tom errado destrói a melhor pergunta
"100%" foca cérebro em imperfeições
"Fairly decent" = posição neutra estratégica
"Making you pay" = linguagem de força/obrigação
Não comprar história do prospect = oferecer valor real
DURAÇÃO: 46:01 minutos FORMATO: Palestra ao vivo com role plays, demonstrações práticas e participação da audiência
